chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ عَلَى الْجِيرَانِ لِأَهْلِ الْمُصِيبَةِ وَ اتِّخَاذِ الْمَأْتَمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 79 – What is Obligated upon the neighbour of the bereaved, and adopting the mourning

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/167)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/167)
text: أَوْصَى أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) بِثَمَانِمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ لِمَأْتَمِهِ وَ كَانَ يَرَى ذَلِكَ مِنَ السُّنَّةِ لِأَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ اتَّخِذُوا لآِلِ جَعْفَرٍ طَعَاماً فَقَدْ شُغِلُوا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, or someone else who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> bequeathed with three hundred Dirhams for mourning him<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> used to view that to be from the Sunnah, because Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Take food to the family of Ja’far<sup>as</sup>, for they are too pre-occupied (with their grief)’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Hariz or others who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, said in his will that eight hundred dirhams should be spent to organize a memorial service for his death. He considered it as part of the tradition; the Messenger of Allah had said, ‘Prepare food for the family of Ja‘far; they are preoccupied (with the death of Ja‘far).’”
